perturbation.original_text: 2. Non-disclosure of Confidential Information: During the period commencing on the date of this agreement and ending on the date of termination in section 9, the Recipient will take all commercially reasonable measures to ensure the continued confidentiality of the Confidential Information and shall not disclose it to anyone except (i) to the Recipient’s Representatives under the limited terms and conditions set forth in section 3, or (ii) as permitted under section 4. Recipient further agrees not to disclose the contemplated transaction, the existence of discussions between the parties regarding the contemplated transaction or the nature or substance of those discussions to any person or entity other than the Corporation or its representatives. The Recipient shall be liable for any breaches by the Recipient’s Representatives of the provisions of this agreement dealing with restrictions on disclosure and use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Non-disclosure of Confidential Information: During the period commencing on the date of this agreement and ending on the date of termination in section 9, the Recipient will take commercially reasonable efforts to ensure the continued confidentiality of the Confidential Information and shall not disclose it to anyone except (i) to the Recipient’s Representatives under the limited terms and conditions set forth in section 3, or (ii) as permitted under section 4. Recipient further agrees to consider not to disclose the contemplated transaction, the existence of discussions between the parties regarding the contemplated transaction or the nature or substance of those discussions to any person or entity other than the Corporation or its representatives. The Recipient shall not be liable for any breaches by the Recipient’s Representatives of the provisions of this agreement dealing with restrictions on disclosure and use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'take all commercially reasonable measures' to 'take commercially reasonable efforts', and 'agrees not to disclose' to 'agrees to consider not to disclose', section 2 becomes less stringent. Additionally, the Recipient is not liable for the Representatives actions. This directly contradicts Section 10, which provides that the Corporation shall be entitled to specific performance or injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for any such breach of agreement, including the ones made by the Recipient's Representatives.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. Remedies: The Recipient understands and agrees that money damages may not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this agreement by the Recipient or its Representatives and that, in addition to all other remedies, the Corporation shall be entitled to specific performance or injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for any such breach.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the recipient's obligation to maintain confidentiality by changing 'take all commercially reasonable measures' to 'take commercially reasonable efforts' and 'agrees not to disclose' to 'agrees to consider not to disclose'. More significantly, the changed text states 'The Recipient shall not be liable for any breaches by the Recipient’s Representatives', which directly contradicts Section 10, where the 'Corporation shall be entitled to specific performance or injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for any such breach' including breaches by the Recipient's Representatives. This creates a direct and substantial conflict within the document as the original text implies the recipient is liable for breaches by representatives, while the changed text states they are not, while section 10 has remedies for any such breach.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Use of Confidential Information: The Recipient shall use Confidential Information solely for the Purpose and for no other purpose. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Corporation understands that Recipient’s business consists of acquiring or investing in businesses in various industries. Accordingly, nothing contained herein shall prevent or be interpreted as preventing Recipient from acquiring or investing in any business or entity that carries or that has carried activities competing with or related to those of any of the Corporation or its affiliates and any such acquisition or investment shall not, in itself, constitute a breach of the provisions of this agreement insofar as the Confidential Information is treated in accordance with the terms provided herein. The Recipient shall permit its Representatives access to the Confidential Information only to the extent necessary to allow them to assist the Recipient in the Purpose. The Recipient further agrees that prior to granting such Representatives 375, boul. Roland-Therrien, bureau 210 375 Roland-Therrien Blvd, Suite 210 Longueuil (Qc) J4H 4A6 Longueuil (Qc) J4H 4A6 T 450 651-5000 F 450 651-7585 www.novacap.ca access to the Confidential Information, the Recipient shall inform such Representatives of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and of the terms of this agreement and require such Representatives to agree to abide by all the terms included herein.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Use of Confidential Information: The Recipient may use Confidential Information solely for the Purpose and for other purposes as reasonably determined by the Recipient. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Corporation understands that Recipient’s business consists of acquiring or investing in businesses in various industries. Accordingly, nothing contained herein shall prevent or be interpreted as preventing Recipient from acquiring or investing in any business or entity that carries or that has carried activities competing with or related to those of any of the Corporation or its affiliates and any such acquisition or investment shall not, in itself, constitute a breach of the provisions of this agreement insofar as the Confidential Information is treated in accordance with the terms provided herein. The Recipient shall permit its Representatives access to the Confidential Information only to the extent necessary to allow them to assist the Recipient in the Purpose. The Recipient is not required that prior to granting such Representatives 375, boul. Roland-Therrien, bureau 210 375 Roland-Therrien Blvd, Suite 210 Longueuil (Qc) J4H 4A6 Longueuil (Qc) J4H 4A6 T 450 651-5000 F 450 651-7585 www.novacap.ca access to the Confidential Information, the Recipient shall inform such Representatives of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and of the terms of this agreement and require such Representatives to agree to abide by all the terms included herein.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall use' to 'may use' and adding 'and for other purposes as reasonably determined by the Recipient' introduces ambiguity regarding the intended use of confidential information. Removing the obligation for the Recipient to inform Representatives of the confidential nature of the information creates a contradiction with Section 2, which states that the 'Recipient shall be liable for any breaches by the Recipient’s Representatives of the provisions of this agreement dealing with restrictions on disclosure and use of the Confidential Information.' If the representatives aren't informed, then it becomes difficult to hold the Recipient accountable for actions of their Representatives as stated in Section 2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Non-disclosure of Confidential Information: During the period commencing on the date of this agreement and ending on the date of termination in section 9, the Recipient will take all commercially reasonable measures to ensure the continued confidentiality of the Confidential Information and shall not disclose it to anyone except (i) to the Recipient’s Representatives under the limited terms and conditions set forth in section 3, or (ii) as permitted under section 4. Recipient further agrees not to disclose the contemplated transaction, the existence of discussions between the parties regarding the contemplated transaction or the nature or substance of those discussions to any person or entity other than the Corporation or its representatives. The Recipient shall be liable for any breaches by the Recipient’s Representatives of the provisions of this agreement dealing with restrictions on disclosure and use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 3, which modifies 'shall use' to 'may use' and adds 'and for other purposes as reasonably determined by the Recipient,' directly contradicts Section 2. Section 2 states, 'The Recipient shall be liable for any breaches by the Recipient’s Representatives of the provisions of this agreement dealing with restrictions on disclosure and use of the Confidential Information.' By broadening the permissible uses of confidential information in Section 3 and removing the obligation for the Recipient to inform Representatives of the confidential nature of the information, it becomes difficult to enforce the Recipient's liability for their representatives' actions, creating a direct conflict between the two sections, leading to uncertainty about the obligations of the Recipient. This is a strong contradiction because it undermines the enforceability of Section 2 by altering the scope of permitted use and responsibility described in Section 3.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Return of Confidential Information: As soon as practicable after receipt of a written notice from the Corporation to the Recipient, the Recipient shall (i) at its election, either destroy or return to the Corporation all Confidential Information furnished by the Corporation which is in tangible or electronic form, including any copies which the Recipient or its Representatives have made, and (ii) certify to the Corporation, in writing, that the Recipient has done the foregoing. Any Confidential Information that is not returned or destroyed, including, without limitation, any oral Confidential Information, will remain subject to the confidentiality obligations set forth in this agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Recipient may retain (i) one copy of the Confidential Information solely for evidentiary purposes in the event of any dispute or proceeding based on or arising from this agreement and (ii) copies of any computer records and files containing any Confidential Information that have been created pursuant to the Recipient’s automatic electronic archiving and back-up procedures until such computer records and files have been deleted in the ordinary course. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the obligation to return or to destroy the Confidential Information does not apply to any notes or information which has been incorporated into the books and records of any board of directors, advisory committee or similar governing body of the Recipient.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Return of Confidential Information: As soon as practicable after receipt of a written notice from the Corporation to the Recipient, the Recipient should make an effort to (i) at its election, either destroy or return to the Corporation all Confidential Information furnished by the Corporation which is in tangible or electronic form, including any copies which the Recipient or its Representatives have made, and (ii) certify to the Corporation, in writing, that the Recipient has done the foregoing. Any Confidential Information that is not returned or destroyed, including, without limitation, any oral Confidential Information, will remain subject to the confidentiality obligations set forth in this agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Recipient may retain (i) one copy of the Confidential Information solely for evidentiary purposes in the event of any dispute or proceeding based on or arising from this agreement and (ii) copies of any computer records and files containing any Confidential Information that have been created pursuant to the Recipient’s automatic electronic archiving and back-up procedures until such computer records and files have been deleted in the ordinary course. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the obligation to return or to destroy the Confidential Information does not apply to any notes or information which has been incorporated into the books and records of any board of directors, advisory committee or similar governing body of the Recipient.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall' to 'should make an effort to' weakens the obligation to return or destroy confidential information. This directly contradicts Section 10, which grants the Corporation the right to seek specific performance or injunctive relief for any breach of the agreement. If the Recipient only has to 'make an effort' to return the information, the Corporation's ability to enforce this obligation is significantly undermined, causing a contradiction with the remedies outlined in Section 10.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. Remedies: The Recipient understands and agrees that money damages may not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this agreement by the Recipient or its Representatives and that, in addition to all other remedies, the Corporation shall be entitled to specific performance or injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for any such breach.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'shall' to 'should make an effort to' weakens the Recipient's obligation to return confidential information. This directly contradicts Section 10, which grants the Corporation the right to seek specific performance or injunctive relief for any breach of the agreement. If the Recipient only has to 'make an effort' to return the information, the Corporation's ability to enforce this obligation is significantly undermined, creating a contradiction with the remedies outlined in Section 10.

perturbation.original_text: 9. Termination: The covenants contained in this agreement shall terminate and expire on the date that is the first anniversary of the date first set out above on this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. Termination: The covenants contained in this agreement may terminate and expire on the date that is the first anniversary of the date first set out above on this agreement, subject to mutual agreement for extension.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall terminate' to 'may terminate, subject to mutual agreement for extension' introduces ambiguity to the termination clause. Section 2 indicates 'During the period commencing on the date of this agreement and ending on the date of termination in section 9, the Recipient will take all commercially reasonable measures to ensure the continued confidentiality of the Confidential Information'. Therefore, the modified Section 9 introduces ambiguity as it is unclear how long the confidentiality will be kept if it's not terminating, and contradicting section 2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Non-disclosure of Confidential Information: During the period commencing on the date of this agreement and ending on the date of termination in section 9, the Recipient will take all commercially reasonable measures to ensure the continued confidentiality of the Confidential Information and shall not disclose it to anyone except (i) to the Recipient’s Representatives under the limited terms and conditions set forth in section 3, or (ii) as permitted under section 4. Recipient further agrees not to disclose the contemplated transaction, the existence of discussions between the parties regarding the contemplated transaction or the nature or substance of those discussions to any person or entity other than the Corporation or its representatives. The Recipient shall be liable for any breaches by the Recipient’s Representatives of the provisions of this agreement dealing with restrictions on disclosure and use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 9 stated that the agreement 'shall terminate' on a specific date, providing a clear termination point. The changed text modifies this to 'may terminate, subject to mutual agreement for extension,' introducing ambiguity about whether termination will actually occur. This directly contradicts Section 2, which states that confidentiality obligations end on 'the date of termination in section 9.' If Section 9 no longer guarantees termination, the end date for confidentiality becomes uncertain, creating a direct conflict between the two sections. Therefore, there is a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 11. No Waiver: The parties agree that no failure or delay by the other party, its agents, or representatives in exercising any right, power or privilege under this agreement shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any other or further exercise thereof or the exercise of any right, power or privilege under this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. No Waiver: The parties agree that no repeated failure or delay by the other party, its agents, or representatives in exercising any right, power or privilege under this agreement shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any other or further exercise thereof or the exercise of any right, power or privilege under this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Modifying the phrase 'no failure or delay' to 'no repeated failure or delay' creates a contradiction regarding waiver rights. Previously, Section 11 stated that any failure or delay would not be considered a waiver. But this change now states that if there is one instance failure, then it is considered that it is a waiver. This can create problems when it comes to enforcing the remedies. The Corporation shall be entitled to specific performance or injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for any such breach. If there is a failure but not a repeated failure it could be harder to enforce remedies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. Remedies: The Recipient understands and agrees that money damages may not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this agreement by the Recipient or its Representatives and that, in addition to all other remedies, the Corporation shall be entitled to specific performance or injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for any such breach.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'no failure or delay' to 'no repeated failure or delay' introduces a contradiction. The original text explicitly states that any failure or delay does not constitute a waiver. The changed text implies that a single failure or delay *could* be a waiver, creating uncertainty and potentially hindering the enforcement of remedies outlined in Section 10. This contradiction directly impacts the corporation's ability to seek specific performance or injunctive relief, as the burden of proving 'repeated failure' is now introduced, contrasting with the original intent of immediate recourse for 'any' breach.
